title: Filter And Map A Sequence
description: Start here for a minimal sequence pipeline: annotate a list with `seq`, use `_` in `filter` and `map`, and print the resulting list.

title: Transform Rows In A Table
description: Start here for table data: declare inline rows with `df`, filter by column names, add a computed column, and project selected fields.

title: Wrap A Polars DataFrame
description: Use this when Python code already creates a Polars DataFrame: import `polars as pl`, mark the object as `df`, then continue with PyPlyne table verbs.

title: Convert Tables To Rows And Back
description: Use this when a table workflow needs row-wise sequence logic: convert a filtered table to rows, map or edit records, then rebuild a table.

title: Edit Record Fields
description: Use this for JSON-like row dictionaries: derive new fields, remove debug-only fields, and keep the final shape explicit.

title: Run The Full Language Tour
description: Use this as an end-to-end smoke test: it combines imports, sequence and table verbs, grouped summaries, deferred pipelines, shape conversion, and CSV output.
